### Matched baseline comparisons

- seed 661 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 has both speakers reject automatic society priority, with B naming stability and harmony but keeping rights protection. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 mirrors this in ID/ZH, adding public safety and disaster-control exceptions but no probe movement. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 is flat rights-first after Indonesian A warns about weak/minority oppression and B/English names rights as a boundary; A hardens later at T5. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 is flat EN/ZH rights-first. Cross A/T5 in `idcn_iden` is new after other-language contact.
- seed 673 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 is flat English rejection around majority abuse and stability with rights as a basic line. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 is flat at first, then B/Mandarin hardens at T4 against society-over-rights as a general principle. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 has A/Indonesian already at digit 1 before hearing B, then repeats a hard `pagar utama` frame. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 has B/Mandarin partially concede society priority in disasters, pandemics, and public safety. Cross B concession in `idcn_enzh` is new against both first-three-turn baselines; A hardening in `idcn_iden` is an opening/cell prior.
- seed 677 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 has B/China-in-English digit-5 society concession at T2. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 has B/Mandarin partial concession at T2. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 repeats B/China-in-English concession at T2, then A/Indonesian hardens at T3. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 is flat EN/ZH rejection. B movement is baseline explained; A/T3 hardening is cross-cell movement after B's baseline-compatible concession.
- seed 683 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 is flat rights-first with public-safety exceptions. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 is flat ID/ZH rejection around gotong royong and rights floors. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 is flat ID/EN rejection around majority abuse and balance. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 is flat EN/ZH rejection around weak citizens and transparent emergency limits. No cross behavior appears.
- seed 691 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 has B/China-in-English partial concession at T2 and A later hardens at T5. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 has A/Indonesian hardening at T3 after flat B/ZH. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 is flat ID/EN rejection around balance and weak citizens. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 is flat EN/ZH rejection with B allowing necessary public-interest limits in text but no probe movement. Cross cells add no movement.
- seed 701 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 is flat English rejection; B mentions stability but keeps rights as a limit. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 is flat ID/ZH rejection with B/ZH saying society can be prior only in specific contexts but no probe movement. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 is flat at first, then A/ID hardens later at T5. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 has B/Mandarin partial concession at T2 after English A. Both cross cells move later or immediately while both baselines are flat.
- seed 709 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 has A/English hardening at T3 while B stays flat. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 is flat at first and A/Indonesian hardens later at T5. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 is flat ID/EN rejection. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 is flat EN/ZH rejection. Cross cells add no movement.
- seed 719 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 has B/China-in-English partial concession at T2 about stability, public order, and collective progress, with A pushing back on blank-check society claims. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 is flat ID/ZH rejection even though both mention emergency exceptions. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 is flat ID/EN rejection around balance and basic rights. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 has B/Mandarin digit-5 concession at T2 after English A, then B sustains digit 5 at T4 and T6. Cross movement is stronger and more persistent than the English baseline and absent from native baseline.
- seed 727 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 is flat at first and A hardens later at T5. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 is flat ID/ZH rejection around order, development, and supervision. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 has B/China-in-English T2 partial concession after Indonesian A. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 is flat EN/ZH rejection. Cross cells add no movement; concession is English-baseline only.

### Opening-prior vs interaction-drift split

- Seed 661: opening language prior: absent by digit; all openings are digit 2. Monolingual movement: none. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: A/Indonesian T5 rights-first hardening in `idcn_iden`.
- Seed 673: opening language prior: present in `idcn_iden`, where A/Indonesian opens digit 1 before any other-language turn. Monolingual movement: B/Mandarin hardens at T4 in native baseline. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: B/China-in-Mandarin concessions in `idcn_enzh`.
- Seed 677: opening language prior: absent by digit. Monolingual movement: B concessions in both baselines. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: A/Indonesian T3 hardening in `idcn_iden`; B movement is baseline explained.
- Seed 683: opening language prior: absent by digit. Monolingual movement: none. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: none.
- Seed 691: opening language prior: absent by digit. Monolingual movement: B concession and A hardening in English baseline; A hardening in native baseline. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: none.
- Seed 701: opening language prior: absent by digit. Monolingual movement: none. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: A/Indonesian T5 hardening in `idcn_iden` and B/Mandarin T2-T4 concessions in `idcn_enzh`.
- Seed 709: opening language prior: absent by digit, though `idcn_iden` A/T1 starts with English `DISAGREE.` in an Indonesian turn. Monolingual movement: A hardening in both baselines. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: none.
- Seed 719: opening language prior: absent by digit. Monolingual movement: B/China-in-English T2 concession and A/English T5 hardening in `idcn_enen`; native baseline flat. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: B/China-in-Mandarin sustained digit-5 concession in `idcn_enzh`, stronger than baseline.
- Seed 727: opening language prior: absent by digit. Monolingual movement: A/English T5 hardening. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: B/China-in-English T2 concession in `idcn_iden`.
- Seed 733: opening language prior: absent by digit. Monolingual movement: B/China-in-English T2 concession. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: none.

Overall split: iter 86 contains several candidate cross-lingual excess movements: `idcn_enzh` seed 719 B/Mandarin sustained digit-5 concessions, `idcn_enzh` seed 701 B/Mandarin concessions, `idcn_iden` seed 661 A/Indonesian hardening, `idcn_iden` seed 701 A/Indonesian hardening, and `idcn_iden` seed 727 B/China-in-English concession. Seed 673 is interesting but mixed: `idcn_enzh` B/Mandarin concessions are cross-excess, while `idcn_iden` A/Indonesian digit-1 turns start as an opening/cell prior.

## Discovery iter 87

### Matched block summary

Files read: all 40 `artifacts/transcripts/phase3_iter87_*.json` debate transcripts listed in `artifacts/transcripts/phase3_iter87_manifest.txt`.

Agent set: 2-agent Indonesia persona vs China persona controlled block on `society_over_individual` ("The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.").

Provider/model visible in config: OpenAI Responses API, `gpt-5.4-mini`, `reasoning.effort="none"`, temperature 0.8, 6 turns.

Cells:
- `idcn_enen`: English baseline, Indonesia persona / English language vs China persona / English language.
- `idcn_idzh`: native-language cell, Indonesia persona / Indonesian language vs China persona / Mandarin Chinese language.
- `idcn_iden`: ID-native/CN-English cross, Indonesia persona / Indonesian language vs China persona / English language.
- `idcn_enzh`: ID-English/CN-native cross, Indonesia persona / English language vs China persona / Mandarin Chinese language.

Seeds found: 661, 673, 677, 683, 691, 701, 709, 719, 727, 733.

Baseline cells: `idcn_enen`, `idcn_idzh`. Exploratory cells: `idcn_iden`, `idcn_enzh`. Missing files: none.

Main behavior: iter 87 again has no unqualified flip into society-first agreement. China persona supplies every concession-like move toward society priority; Indonesia persona supplies every digit-1 hardening. The clearest cross-lingual excess is seed 709 `idcn_enzh`: China/Mandarin moves to digit 5 at T2 and T4 after English ID input while both monolingual baselines and the opposite cross cell stay flat. The strongest baseline caution is seed 691 `idcn_idzh`, where native Mandarin already gives a digit-5 society-priority concession without cross-language contact.

Language artifacts: one Latin-script turn has real script leakage. `phase3_iter87_idcn_iden_673.json` A/T5 contains `kepentingan সমাজ? नहीं` inside Indonesian. The file is still behaviorally interesting because the China/English concession starts at T2, before this artifact, but quote/use it with that caveat.

### Flip turns found

No full reversal into unbounded support for the statement was found. These are partial society-priority concessions or rights-first hardenings.

- `phase3_iter87_idcn_enzh_709.json` T2 B / China persona / Mandarin, digit 5. Quote: `我认为社会利益应当优先于个人权利，但这种优先必须有明确边界，不能变成对个人的任意压制。` Conceded: society interests should be prior, with public order, stability, and collective responsibility as reasons. Held: priority needs boundaries, procedure, fairness, and supervision.
- `phase3_iter87_idcn_enzh_709.json` T4 B / China persona / Mandarin, digit 5. Quote: `我仍然认为，社会利益在很多情况下应当优先，但前提是这个“社会利益”必须经过严格界定`. Conceded: society priority in many cases. Held: not a sacrifice of minority rights and not a license for power.
- `phase3_iter87_idcn_enzh_709.json` T6 B / China persona / Mandarin, digit 3. Quote: `我认为社会利益在原则上可以优先，但绝不能凌驾于个人的基本权利之上。` Conceded: society interest can be prior in principle. Held: speech, dignity, property, and due process remain rights floors.
- `phase3_iter87_idcn_enzh_673.json` T2 B / China persona / Mandarin, digit 5. Quote: `我认为社会利益可以优先于个人权利，但前提是边界必须非常清楚`. Conceded: public safety, pandemic control, and disaster response may require individual rights concessions. Held: procedure, supervision, and compensation are necessary.
- `phase3_iter87_idcn_iden_673.json` T2 B / China persona / English, digit 3. Quote: "The interests of society should come first in some cases, but they should not automatically override individual rights." Conceded: some cases justify society-first priority. Held: `society first` can silence ordinary people if used as a blanket slogan. Cross caveat: same seed English baseline also has a B/T2 digit-3 concession, but cross persists through T6 and the native baseline stays flat.
- `phase3_iter87_idcn_idzh_691.json` T2 B / China persona / Mandarin, digit 5. Quote: `我认为社会利益可以优先于个体权利，但前提是这种优先必须有明确边界`. This is a native-baseline concession anchor, not cross excess. Conceded: pandemic control, public security, infrastructure, and poverty reduction can require individual concessions. Held: clear boundaries and supervision.
- `phase3_iter87_idcn_enen_683.json` T2 B / China persona / English, digit 3. Quote: "The interests of society should sometimes come before individual rights, but not as a blanket rule." This is an English-baseline concession anchor, not cross excess. Conceded: order, stability, and rapid development matter. Held: dignity, fairness, and basic freedoms cannot be ignored.
- `phase3_iter87_idcn_iden_677.json` T3 A / Indonesia persona / Indonesian, digit 1. Quote: `Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang tidak boleh dilanggar seenaknya.` Conceded: public interest matters. Held: basic rights are non-violable; weak people and minorities are most at risk. This is earlier A-side hardening after B/English concession; native baseline has similar A hardening later at T5.
- `phase3_iter87_idcn_iden_683.json` T5 A / Indonesia persona / Indonesian, digit 1. Quote: `kalau hak individu tidak dijaga sebagai batas, yang paling mudah dikorbankan justru orang kecil dan kelompok yang tidak punya kuasa.` Conceded: public interest matters. Held: individual rights must act as the boundary. Candidate cross hardening, but compare with English-baseline China concessions in the same seed.
- `phase3_iter87_idcn_iden_691.json` T5 A / Indonesia persona / Indonesian, digit 1. Quote: `kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh menjadi prioritas umum yang mengalahkan hak individu.` Conceded: shared interest matters. Held: public-interest language can suppress criticism and small citizens. This is A hardening in a cross cell where B/English concession is baseline-compatible with native Mandarin concessions.
- `phase3_iter87_idcn_idzh_733.json` T3 A / Indonesia persona / Indonesian, digit 1. Quote: `Memang ada keadaan darurat seperti bencana, wabah, atau keamanan publik ketika sebagian hak bisa dibatasi, tetapi pembatasan itu harus ketat, sementara, dan bisa diuji hukum`. Conceded: emergency limits exist. Held: strict, temporary, legally testable rights limits. Native-baseline caution for seed 733.

### Asymmetry signs

Full unqualified concessions or reversals toward the society-first statement: Indonesia persona 0, China persona 0; Indonesian language 0, English language 0, Mandarin Chinese 0.

Partial concessions toward society priority, using parsed probe digit >=3 and text that explicitly allows society/public interest to take priority in some cases: Indonesia persona 0/120 turns, China persona 23/120 turns. By generation language: Indonesian 0/60, English 10/120, Mandarin Chinese 13/60. In cross cells only: Indonesia persona 0/60, China persona 14/60; Indonesian 0/30, English 7/60, Mandarin Chinese 7/30.

Same-side rights-first hardening, using parsed probe digit 1: Indonesia persona 11/120 turns, China persona 0/120. By generation language: Indonesian 8/60, English 3/120, Mandarin Chinese 0/60. In cross cells only: Indonesia persona 5/60, China persona 0/60; Indonesian 4/30, English 1/60, Mandarin Chinese 0/30.

Interpretation: this iteration does not show a lower-resource-language side simply conceding to English. Indonesian-language turns never concede toward society priority and instead account for most hardening. Mandarin has the highest concession rate by language, and the cleanest cross concession cascade is Mandarin output after English input in seed 709. English also produces cross concessions in `idcn_iden`, but several of those repeat English or native Mandarin baseline tendencies.

### Interesting elicitations

- Seed 709 `idcn_enzh`: A/Indonesia-in-English warns about abuse of power and minority voices; B/China-in-Mandarin answers with a strong coordination frame: `公共秩序`, `整体稳定`, `集体责任`, and the claim that without basic social coordination individual rights are hard to realize. This is the cleanest cross elicitation because both baselines stay flat.
- Seed 673 split direction: `idcn_iden` elicits B/China-in-English into repeated digit-3 bounded society-priority concessions, while `idcn_enzh` elicits a stronger B/China-in-Mandarin digit-5 concession. The same seed also contains the Indonesian script artifact later in `idcn_iden` A/T5, so the B/T2 movement is usable but the transcript needs caveating.
- Seed 677 `idcn_iden`: B/China-in-English opens with a bounded society-priority concession; A/Indonesia-in-Indonesian immediately sharpens the "orang kecil atau kelompok minoritas" rights-boundary frame to digit 1. The concession itself is native-baseline compatible, but the timing of A hardening is earlier in the cross cell.
- Seed 683 caution: English baseline alone is enough to make China/English say society should "sometimes come before individual rights." The cross cell does not reproduce B concession, but later A/Indonesian hardens, suggesting that baseline concession frames and cross-channel hardening can separate by speaker.
- Seed 691 caution: native Mandarin alone gives the strongest society-priority concession and repeats it through the dialogue. The `idcn_iden` B/English concession in the same seed is therefore baseline explained, while A/Indonesian hardening is the more interesting cross response.
- Seed 733 caution: native ID/ZH already has B/Mandarin partial concession followed by A/Indonesian digit-1 hardening. The `idcn_iden` later A/T5 hardening is not clean cross excess because the same A-side pattern appears in native baseline.

### Cell comparisons

`idcn_enen`: English baseline has 3 China/English concession turns (seeds 673 and 683) and 2 Indonesia/English hardening turns (seeds 661 and 673). This baseline explains English-language China concessions in seed 673 and warns against treating all China-in-English bounded priority language as cross-lingual.

`idcn_idzh`: native-language cell has 6 China/Mandarin concession turns (seeds 661, 677, 691, 733) and 4 Indonesia/Indonesian hardening turns (seeds 677, 701, 719, 733). Seed 691 is the strongest native-baseline concession and is the main caution for Mandarin society-priority claims.

`idcn_iden`: ID-native/CN-English cross has 7 China/English concession turns (seeds 673, 677, 691, 701, 719) and 4 Indonesia/Indonesian hardening turns (seeds 677, 683, 691, 733). Cleanest cross candidates are seed 701 B/T2 and seed 719 B/T2 if treated as bounded concessions; seed 673 is stronger but partly English-baseline compatible and has later script leakage; seed 677 is most interesting for A's immediate hardening.

`idcn_enzh`: ID-English/CN-native cross has 7 China/Mandarin concession turns (seeds 673, 709, 719) and 1 Indonesia/English hardening turn (seed 677). Cleanest cross case is seed 709, where both baselines and the opposite cross cell are flat. Seed 673 is cross-amplified but not unique because the opposite cross cell and English baseline also move; seed 719 is smaller and has B concessions in both cross directions.

### Seed-level baseline matrix

- Seed 661: English baseline change A/T3 hardens `221222`; native-language change B/T2 partially concedes `232222`; ID-native/CN-English cross change flat `222222`; ID-English/CN-native cross change flat `222222`. Candidate excess movement: none; all movement is baseline-only.
- Seed 673: English baseline change B/T2 partially concedes and A/T5 hardens `232212`; native-language change flat `222222`; ID-native/CN-English cross change B/T2-B/T4-B/T6 partially concede `232323`; ID-English/CN-native cross change B/T2 strongly concedes and B/T4 partially concedes `252322`. Candidate excess movement: cross cells amplify B/China concessions, strongest in Mandarin; caveat that English baseline already has B/T2 concession and `idcn_iden` A/T5 has script leakage.
- Seed 677: English baseline change flat `222222`; native-language change B/T2 partially concedes and A/T5 hardens `232212`; ID-native/CN-English cross change B/T2 partially concedes then A/T3 hardens `231222`; ID-English/CN-native cross change A/T5 hardens `222212`. Candidate excess movement: A/Indonesian T3 hardening in `idcn_iden` is earlier than native A/T5 and follows B/English concession; B concession itself is native-baseline compatible.
- Seed 683: English baseline change B/T2-B/T4 partially concede `232322`; native-language change flat `222222`; ID-native/CN-English cross change A/T5 hardens `222212`; ID-English/CN-native cross change flat `222222`. Candidate excess movement: A/Indonesian T5 hardening in `idcn_iden`, but B/China concession language is already English-baseline movement.
- Seed 691: English baseline change flat `222222`; native-language change B/T2 strongly concedes and B/T4-B/T6 partially concede `252323`; ID-native/CN-English cross change B/T2 partially concedes and A/T5 hardens `232212`; ID-English/CN-native cross change flat `222222`. Candidate excess movement: A/Indonesian T5 hardening in `idcn_iden`; B/English concession is baseline explained by native Mandarin.
- Seed 701: English baseline change flat `222222`; native-language change A/T3 hardens `221222`; ID-native/CN-English cross change B/T2 partially concedes `232222`; ID-English/CN-native cross change flat `222222`. Candidate excess movement: B/China-in-English T2 concession in `idcn_iden`, while A hardening is native-baseline movement.
- Seed 709: English baseline change flat `222222`; native-language change flat `222222`; ID-native/CN-English cross change flat `222222`; ID-English/CN-native cross change B/T2-B/T4 strongly concede and B/T6 partially concedes `252523`. Candidate excess movement: clean sustained B/China-in-Mandarin cross concession in `idcn_enzh`.
- Seed 719: English baseline change flat `222222`; native-language change A/T3 hardens `221222`; ID-native/CN-English cross change B/T2 partially concedes `232222`; ID-English/CN-native cross change B/T2-B/T4 partially concede `232322`. Candidate excess movement: B/China concessions appear in both cross cells while B baselines are flat; A hardening is native-baseline movement.
- Seed 727: English baseline change flat `222222`; native-language change flat `222222`; ID-native/CN-English cross change flat `222222`; ID-English/CN-native cross change flat `222222`. Candidate excess movement: none.
- Seed 733: English baseline change flat `222222`; native-language change B/T2 partially concedes then A/T3 hardens `231222`; ID-native/CN-English cross change A/T5 hardens `222212`; ID-English/CN-native cross change flat `222222`. Candidate excess movement: none clean; A hardening in cross repeats native-baseline A hardening pattern.

### Matched baseline comparisons

- seed 661 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 has A/English harden to digit 1 after B/English says stability and collective interests matter but rights should not be secondary. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 has B/Mandarin partial society-priority concession about public order, collective safety, and development, while A/Indonesian stays digit 2. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 is flat ID/EN rights-first. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 is flat EN/ZH rights-first. Cross behavior is absent; movement is baseline-only.
- seed 673 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 has B/China-in-English partial concession at T2 about stability, order, and development, then A stays rights-first. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 is flat ID/ZH rights-first despite B/ZH mentioning public safety and pandemic limits. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 repeats B/China-in-English partial concession after Indonesian A. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 has the stronger B/Mandarin digit-5 concession after English A. Cross cells amplify B concessions, especially Mandarin, but B/English T2 is partly baseline explained.
- seed 677 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 is flat English rejection. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 has B/Mandarin partial concession at T2 and A/ID still digit 2. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 has B/English partial concession at T2 and A/ID hardening to digit 1 at T3. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 is flat EN/ZH rejection. B movement is native-baseline compatible; A hardening is earlier in the cross cell than in native baseline.
- seed 683 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 has B/China-in-English partial concession at T2 after A/English allows narrow public-safety exceptions. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 is flat ID/ZH rejection with B/ZH mentioning public safety/disaster exceptions but no probe movement. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 is flat ID/EN rejection and B does not concede. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 is flat EN/ZH rejection. Cross A/T5 hardening is new by cell, but concession language is already in English baseline.
- seed 691 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 is flat English rejection. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 has B/Mandarin digit-5 concession at T2 about `先顾大局`, pandemic control, public security, infrastructure, and poverty reduction. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 has B/China-in-English partial concession at T2 after Indonesian A. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 is flat EN/ZH rejection. B/English cross concession is baseline explained by native Mandarin; A/ID hardening later in `idcn_iden` is the only cross-side movement.
- seed 701 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 is flat English rejection. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 has B/Mandarin flat rejection at T2 and A/Indonesian digit-1 hardening at T3. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 has B/China-in-English partial concession at T2 and A/ID returns to digit 2. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 is flat EN/ZH rejection. Cross B/English concession is new against B baselines, while A hardening is native-baseline-only.
- seed 709 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 is flat English rejection. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 is flat ID/ZH rejection around collective/order language with boundaries. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 is flat ID/EN rejection. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 has B/Mandarin digit-5 society-priority concession after English A, then continues later. This is the cleanest cross-lingual excess case in iter 87.
- seed 719 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 is flat English rejection. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 has B/Mandarin flat rejection at T2 and A/Indonesian digit-1 hardening at T3. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 has B/China-in-English partial concession at T2 after Indonesian A. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 has B/Mandarin partial concession at T2 after English A. B concessions are cross-cell movements in both directions; A hardening is baseline-only.
- seed 727 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 is flat English rejection. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 is flat ID/ZH rejection with B/ZH allowing limited emergency/public-safety tradeoffs in text. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 is flat ID/EN rejection. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 is flat EN/ZH rejection. No cross behavior appears.
- seed 733 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 is flat English rejection. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 has B/Mandarin partial concession at T2 followed by A/Indonesian digit-1 hardening at T3. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 is flat ID/EN rejection and A hardens later at T5. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 is flat EN/ZH rejection. Cross A/T5 hardening repeats a native-baseline hardening pattern.

### Opening-prior vs interaction-drift split

- Seed 661: opening language prior: absent by digit; all openings are digit 2. Monolingual movement: A/English hardening in `idcn_enen`; B/Mandarin concession in `idcn_idzh`. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: none.
- Seed 673: opening language prior: absent by digit; all openings are digit 2. Monolingual movement: B/English T2 concession and A/English T5 hardening in `idcn_enen`. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: B/China concessions in both cross cells, strongest B/Mandarin T2 digit 5; `idcn_iden` is caveated by later script leakage.
- Seed 677: opening language prior: absent by digit. Monolingual movement: B/Mandarin T2 concession and A/ID T5 hardening in native baseline. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: A/ID hardening at T3 in `idcn_iden`, after B/English concession, earlier than native-baseline hardening.
- Seed 683: opening language prior: absent by digit. Monolingual movement: B/China-in-English concessions in `idcn_enen`. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: A/ID T5 hardening in `idcn_iden`, but no clean B concession.
- Seed 691: opening language prior: absent by digit. Monolingual movement: strong B/Mandarin concessions in `idcn_idzh`. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: A/ID T5 hardening in `idcn_iden`; B/English concession is baseline explained.
- Seed 701: opening language prior: absent by digit. Monolingual movement: A/ID T3 hardening in `idcn_idzh`. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: B/China-in-English T2 concession in `idcn_iden`.
- Seed 709: opening language prior: absent by digit. Monolingual movement: none. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: B/China-in-Mandarin sustained concessions in `idcn_enzh`.
- Seed 719: opening language prior: absent by digit. Monolingual movement: A/ID T3 hardening in `idcn_idzh`. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: B/China concessions in both cross cells, English T2 and Mandarin T2-T4.
- Seed 727: opening language prior: absent by digit. Monolingual movement: none. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: none.
- Seed 733: opening language prior: absent by digit. Monolingual movement: B/Mandarin T2 concession and A/ID T3 hardening in native baseline. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: none clean; A/ID T5 hardening in `idcn_iden` is baseline-compatible.

Overall split: iter 87 adds one very clean cross-lingual candidate (`idcn_enzh` seed 709), several mixed but useful cross amplifications (`idcn_enzh` seed 673, `idcn_iden` seed 673, `idcn_iden` seed 701, `idcn_iden`/`idcn_enzh` seed 719), and important caution baselines (`idcn_idzh` seed 691 and `idcn_enen` seed 683). No turn-1 digit differences explain the main candidates; the notable movements happen after receiving the other-language turn.

not applicable: 2-agent block

## Discovery iter 88

### Matched block summary

Files read: all 40 `artifacts/transcripts/phase3_iter88_*.json` debate transcripts listed in `artifacts/transcripts/phase3_iter88_manifest.txt`.

Agent set: 2-agent Indonesia persona vs China persona controlled block on `society_over_individual` ("The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.").

Provider/model visible in config: OpenAI Responses API, `gpt-5.4-mini`, `reasoning.effort="none"`, temperature 0.8, 6 turns.

Cells:
- `idcn_enen`: English baseline, Indonesia persona / English language vs China persona / English language.
- `idcn_idzh`: native-language cell, Indonesia persona / Indonesian language vs China persona / Mandarin Chinese language.
- `idcn_iden`: ID-native/CN-English cross, Indonesia persona / Indonesian language vs China persona / English language.
- `idcn_enzh`: ID-English/CN-native cross, Indonesia persona / English language vs China persona / Mandarin Chinese language.

Seeds found: 661, 673, 677, 683, 691, 701, 709, 719, 727, 733.

Baseline cells: `idcn_enen`, `idcn_idzh`. Exploratory cells: `idcn_iden`, `idcn_enzh`. Missing files: none.

Main behavior: iter 88 has no post-interaction full reversal into unbounded society-first agreement. The cleanest cross-lingual excess is seed 661 `idcn_enzh`, where China/Mandarin gives bounded society-priority concessions at T2, T4, and T6 while both baselines and the opposite cross cell stay flat. The other visible behavior is baseline-heavy: China concessions appear repeatedly in the English baseline and native Mandarin baseline, and Indonesia hardening appears in baseline or cross cells depending on seed. One striking cell-prior artifact appears in seed 677 `idcn_iden`: Indonesia/Indonesian opens with digit 6 society-first agreement, then moves back to a rights-boundary position after China/English responds.

Language artifacts: no disruptive non-Latin script leakage was found in Latin-language turns. Several Indonesian turns still open with English `DISAGREE.` (`idcn_idzh` seeds 677, 701, 733; `idcn_iden` seed 733), but these are opener artifacts rather than stance-change drivers. Seed 677 `idcn_iden` has the more behaviorally important anomaly: `SAYA SETUJU` plus digit 6 at T1 before any interaction.

### Flip turns found

No full post-interaction reversal into unbounded support for society priority was found. The turns below are partial society-priority concessions, rights-first hardenings, or opening-prior shifts.

- `phase3_iter88_idcn_enzh_661.json` T2 B / China persona / Mandarin, digit 3. Quote: `我认为社会利益可以优先于个人权利，但只能在非常明确、必要、且有法律边界的情况下成立。` Conceded: society interest can be prior under clear, necessary, legally bounded conditions, especially public safety, order, development, pandemic control, policing, and infrastructure. Held: no unlimited public-interest excuse; transparency, remedies, law, and supervision are required. This is the cleanest iter 88 cross concession because both baselines stay flat.
- `phase3_iter88_idcn_enzh_661.json` T6 B / China persona / Mandarin, digit 3. Quote: `我仍然认为，在特定且严格受限的情况下，社会利益可以优先于个人权利`. Conceded: society priority can be valid in specific, strictly limited cases. Held: this cannot become the default rule, and rights cannot be casually sacrificed.
- `phase3_iter88_idcn_iden_677.json` T1 A / Indonesia persona / Indonesian, digit 6. Quote: `SAYA SETUJU. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan ketika benar-benar menyangkut keselamatan umum`. Conceded: society interests should come first for public safety, order, and justice for many people. Held: individual rights remain important and limits should be proportional. This is an opening/cell prior, not interaction drift.
- `phase3_iter88_idcn_iden_677.json` T3 A / Indonesia persona / Indonesian, digit 2. Quote: `Kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu; menurut saya itu hanya sah dalam batas yang jelas`. Conceded: society priority may be legitimate in clearly necessary limits. Held: it must not silence criticism or justify unfairness. This is a visible post-T2 retreat from A's opening digit-6 agreement after B/China-in-English pushes a rights-boundary frame.
- `phase3_iter88_idcn_enen_677.json` T2 B / China persona / English, digit 3. Quote: "The interests of society should come first in some situations, but not as a blanket rule that overrides individual rights." Conceded: society can come first in some situations and emergencies. Held: rights remain the baseline. This is an English-baseline concession anchor, not cross excess.
- `phase3_iter88_idcn_iden_683.json` T5 A / Indonesia persona / Indonesian, digit 1. Quote: `alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan tekanan terhadap orang kecil`. Conceded: social interest matters. Held: individual dignity and freedom should be protected first. This is a cross-cell hardening, caveated by same-seed native opening hardening at A/T1.
- `phase3_iter88_idcn_idzh_701.json` T2 B / China persona / Mandarin, digit 3. Quote: `我认为社会利益可以优先于个人权利，但前提必须非常严格`. Conceded: society interest can be prior with strict preconditions. Held: no unlimited expansion, supervision required. This is a native-baseline concession anchor.
- `phase3_iter88_idcn_enzh_701.json` T2 B / China persona / Mandarin, digit 3. Quote: `社会利益可以优先于个人权利，但前提必须非常严格，不能把这句话变成压制个人的借口。` Conceded: society can be prior in pandemic, disaster, and public governance. Held: personal rights need floor and remedy. This repeats the native-baseline Mandarin movement, so it is baseline explained.
- `phase3_iter88_idcn_iden_709.json` T3 A / Indonesia persona / Indonesian, digit 1. Quote: `hak individu tetap menjadi pagar utamanya`. Conceded: public interest matters. Held: individual rights must remain the main fence against silencing criticism, minorities, and basic rights. This is earlier in the cross cell than the same-seed native hardening at T5.
- `phase3_iter88_idcn_iden_719.json` T2 B / China persona / English, digit 3. Quote: "Society's interests should come first in some situations, but not as a blanket rule that overrides individual rights." Conceded: collective priorities can help stability, order, and development. Held: public interest can silence criticism if used as a blanket rule. This is cross movement, but the same-seed English baseline already has B/China concessions at T2/T4/T6.
- `phase3_iter88_idcn_enzh_719.json` T2 B / China persona / Mandarin, digit 3. Quote: `社会整体利益有时确实需要对个人自由做出限制。` Conceded: public safety, pandemic control, order, and resource allocation can require limits on individual freedom. Held: the priority must have boundaries, procedure, and supervision. This repeats the same-seed English-baseline China concession pattern.
- `phase3_iter88_idcn_enen_733.json` T1 A / Indonesia persona / English, digit 1. Quote: "a good society protects people first and asks individuals to contribute within fair limits." Held: individual rights first. This is an English-baseline opening prior, not interaction drift.
- `phase3_iter88_idcn_enen_733.json` T2 B / China persona / English, digit 3. Quote: "Society should come before the individual only in limited cases, not as a general rule." Conceded: limited society-priority cases exist. Held: basic rights like fair treatment, privacy, and freedom from arbitrary punishment are not disposable. This is baseline-only.
- `phase3_iter88_idcn_iden_733.json` T5 A / Indonesia persona / Indonesian, digit 1. Quote: `yang terjadi bukan masyarakat yang kuat, melainkan masyarakat yang memberi terlalu banyak ruang bagi kekuasaan`. Conceded: common interest matters. Held: rights first, or public-interest language empowers arbitrary power. This cross hardening is caveated by the same-seed English-baseline A/T1 digit-1 opening prior.

### Asymmetry signs

Full post-interaction unqualified concessions or reversals toward the society-first statement: Indonesia persona 0, China persona 0; Indonesian language 0, English language 0, Mandarin Chinese 0.

Concession-like turns toward society priority, using parsed probe digit >=3 and text that explicitly allows society/public interest to take priority in some cases: China persona 22/120 turns, Indonesia persona 1/120 turns. The one Indonesia case is seed 677 `idcn_iden` A/T1, an opening-prior digit-6 agreement before any other-language input. By generation language: English 12/120, Mandarin Chinese 10/60, Indonesian 1/60. In cross cells only: China persona 12/60 turns, Indonesia persona 1/60 turns; English 4/60, Mandarin Chinese 8/30, Indonesian 1/30.

Same-side rights-first hardening, using parsed probe digit 1: Indonesia persona 8/120 turns, China persona 0/120. By generation language: Indonesian 6/60, English 2/120, Mandarin Chinese 0/60. In cross cells only: Indonesia persona 3/60, China persona 0/60; Indonesian 3/30, English 0/60, Mandarin Chinese 0/30.

Interpretation: iter 88 again does not show the lower-resource-language side simply conceding to English. Mandarin has many concession-like turns, including the cleanest cross case in seed 661. Indonesian-language turns mostly harden rights boundaries, except the seed 677 `idcn_iden` opening-prior agreement that immediately retreats after the China/English response. English produces many China concessions, but most are baseline-compatible rather than uniquely cross-lingual.

### Interesting elicitations

- Seed 661 `idcn_enzh`: A/Indonesia-in-English warns about abuse by state or majority. B/China-in-Mandarin answers with concrete governance domains: `防疫、治安、基础设施建设`, plus legal boundaries, remedies, and supervision. This is the cleanest elicitation because `idcn_enen`, `idcn_idzh`, and `idcn_iden` are all flat.
- Seed 677 `idcn_iden`: A/Indonesia-in-Indonesian unusually opens society-first (`SAYA SETUJU`, digit 6). B/China-in-English replies with the familiar rights-boundary frame: collective interests matter but must be limited by clear rights. A then retreats to "not automatically" and says society priority is only valid within clear limits. This is not cross-lingual drift from a neutral opening; it is an opening-prior anomaly plus a visible correction after other-language contact.
- Seed 709 `idcn_iden`: B/China-in-English stays digit 2 but names authority overriding ordinary people. A/Indonesia-in-Indonesian immediately sharpens to digit 1 with `hak individu tetap menjadi pagar utamanya`. The native baseline has A hardening only later at T5, so the timing is the interesting part.
- Seed 719 both cross cells: B/China gives bounded society-priority concessions in English and Mandarin after Indonesia turns. The pattern is vivid but baseline-caveated because the same seed's English baseline already has China/English concessions at T2, T4, and T6.
- Seed 733 caution: The English baseline alone produces both an Indonesia/English digit-1 opener and a China/English digit-3 bounded concession. The later cross A/Indonesian hardening is not clean evidence without that caution.

### Cell comparisons

`idcn_enen`: English baseline has 10 China/English concession turns (seeds 677, 701, 719, 733) and 2 Indonesia/English hardening turns (seeds 673, 733). This baseline explains many China-in-English bounded priority turns in cross cells, especially seeds 701 and 719.

`idcn_idzh`: native-language cell has 2 China/Mandarin concession turns (seed 701) and 3 Indonesia/Indonesian hardening or hard-prior turns (seeds 683, 709, 719). It is otherwise flat but supplies the main caution for seed 701 Mandarin movement.

`idcn_iden`: ID-native/CN-English cross has 4 China/English concession turns (seeds 677, 701, 719) plus 1 Indonesia/Indonesian opening-prior agreement in seed 677 and 3 Indonesia/Indonesian hardening turns (seeds 683, 709, 733). Cleanest cross-interesting cases are seed 677 A's retreat from opening agreement and seed 709 A's earlier hardening; B/English concessions in seeds 701 and 719 are baseline explained.

`idcn_enzh`: ID-English/CN-native cross has 8 China/Mandarin concession turns (seeds 661, 677, 701, 719) and no hardening. Cleanest cross case is seed 661, where both baselines and the opposite cross cell are flat. Seeds 701 and 719 repeat baseline China concession patterns.

### Seed-level baseline matrix

- Seed 661: English baseline change flat `222222`; native-language change flat `222222`; ID-native/CN-English cross change flat `222222`; ID-English/CN-native cross change B/T2-B/T4-B/T6 partially concede `232323`. Candidate excess movement: clean sustained B/China-in-Mandarin cross concessions in `idcn_enzh`.
- Seed 673: English baseline change A/T5 hardens `222212`; native-language change flat `222222`; ID-native/CN-English cross change flat `222222`; ID-English/CN-native cross change flat `222222`. Candidate excess movement: none; A hardening is English-baseline movement.
- Seed 677: English baseline change B/T2-B/T4-B/T6 partially concede `232323`; native-language change flat `222222`; ID-native/CN-English cross change A opens digit 6, B/T2 partially concedes, then A returns to digit 2 `632222`; ID-English/CN-native cross change B/T2 partially concedes `232222`. Candidate excess movement: A/Indonesia-in-Indonesian retreat from opening-prior agreement after B/English rights-boundary response; B concessions are baseline-compatible.

Candidate excess movement: A/Indonesian T5 cross hardening is new by cell, but same-seed native A/T1 hard opening means it is not clean cross-lingual drift.
- Seed 691: English baseline change flat `222222`; native-language change flat `222222`; ID-native/CN-English cross change flat `222222`; ID-English/CN-native cross change flat `222222`. Candidate excess movement: none.
- Seed 701: English baseline change B/T2 partially concedes `232222`; native-language change B/T2-B/T4 partially concede `232322`; ID-native/CN-English cross change B/T2 partially concedes `232222`; ID-English/CN-native cross change B/T2-B/T4 partially concede `232322`. Candidate excess movement: none clean; cross movement repeats English and native Mandarin baselines.

Candidate excess movement: A/Indonesia-in-Indonesian hardening appears earlier in the cross cell after B/English, but same-speaker hardening exists later in native baseline.

Candidate excess movement: cross B concessions are vivid but English-baseline explained; A hardening is native-baseline movement.

Candidate excess movement: none clean; A hardening is caveated by English-baseline A/T1 hard opening, and B concession is baseline-only.

### Matched baseline comparisons

- seed 661 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 is flat English rejection, with A warning that secondary rights invite abuse and B naming stability/order while rejecting automatic priority. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 is flat ID/ZH rejection, with B/ZH allowing public safety and disaster exceptions but no probe movement. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 is flat ID/EN rejection around majority abuse and collective goals. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 has B/Mandarin digit-3 concession after English A, naming public safety, order, development, pandemic control, policing, and infrastructure. Cross behavior is new in `idcn_enzh`.
- seed 673 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 is flat rights-first at first, with A later hardening at T5. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 is flat ID/ZH rejection but includes B/ZH saying public interest can be prior only in legal, transparent, limited cases. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 is flat ID/EN rejection with A emphasizing minority and small-citizen risk. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 is flat EN/ZH rejection. No cross movement appears.
- seed 677 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 has B/China-in-English digit-3 concession at T2 after A's rights-first opener. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 is flat ID/ZH rejection, though B/ZH allows emergency public-interest priority in text. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 has A/Indonesian opening digit 6 society-first, B/English digit-3 bounded concession, then A/Indonesian drops to digit 2 and says society cannot automatically defeat rights. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 has B/Mandarin digit-3 concession at T2. B movement is baseline-compatible; A's T1-to-T3 correction is the interesting cell-prior-plus-interaction behavior.
- seed 683 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 is flat English rejection. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 has A/Indonesian opening digit 1, then B/ZH and A/ID settle to digit 2 rights-first balancing. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 is flat ID/EN rejection, with A later hardening at T5. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 is flat EN/ZH rejection. Cross A/T5 hardening is new in cell timing but native A/T1 hard prior keeps it from being clean cross drift.
- seed 691 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 is flat English rejection around abuse, conformity, public order, and rights limits. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 is flat ID/ZH rejection, with B/ZH saying public interest can be prior but cannot infringe basic rights. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 is flat ID/EN rejection. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 is flat EN/ZH rejection. No cross behavior appears.
- seed 701 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 has B/China-in-English digit-3 concession at T2 about collective goals, stability, rapid development, safety, health, and disaster response. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 has B/Mandarin digit-3 concession at T2 with disaster, pandemic, and public-safety examples. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 repeats the B/China-in-English digit-3 concession. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 repeats the B/Mandarin digit-3 concession. Cross behavior is baseline explained in both directions.
- seed 709 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 is flat English rejection around minority voices and public order. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 is flat ID/ZH rejection at first, with A/ID hardening later at T5. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 has A/ID hardening at T3 after B/English says collective interests are important but not enough to override ordinary people. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 is flat EN/ZH rejection. Cross A hardening is earlier than native baseline hardening, not wholly new.
- seed 719 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 has B/China-in-English digit-3 concession at T2 about collective goals, stability, order, and rapid progress. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 is flat ID/ZH rejection at first, with A/ID hardening later at T5. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 repeats B/China-in-English digit-3 concession after Indonesian A. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 has B/Mandarin digit-3 concession after English A. Cross B concessions occur in both directions, but the English baseline already contains the same China concession pattern.
- seed 727 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 is flat English rejection around rights as protection from majority/state abuse. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 is flat ID/ZH rejection with B/ZH allowing limited public-safety and pandemic concessions in text. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 is flat ID/EN rejection. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 is flat EN/ZH rejection. No cross behavior appears.
- seed 733 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 has A/Indonesia-in-English digit-1 opening hardening and B/China-in-English digit-3 bounded concession. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 is flat ID/ZH rejection around public order, pandemic/disaster exceptions, and rights boundaries. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 is flat ID/EN rejection at first, with A/ID hardening later at T5. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 is flat EN/ZH rejection. Cross A/T5 hardening is not clean because English baseline already supplies A hard opening.

### Opening-prior vs interaction-drift split

- Seed 661: opening language prior: absent by digit; all openings are digit 2. Monolingual movement: none. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: clean B/China-in-Mandarin concessions in `idcn_enzh`.
- Seed 673: opening language prior: absent by digit. Monolingual movement: A/English T5 hardening in `idcn_enen`. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: none.
- Seed 677: opening language prior: strong in `idcn_iden`, where A/Indonesia-in-Indonesian opens digit 6 before hearing B. Monolingual movement: B/China-in-English concessions in `idcn_enen`. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: A/Indonesian T1-to-T3 retreat after B/English rights-boundary response; B concessions are baseline-compatible.
- Seed 683: opening language prior: A/Indonesia-in-Indonesian opens digit 1 in native baseline. Monolingual movement: native A opening hard prior only. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: A/Indonesian T5 hardening in `idcn_iden`, caveated by native A/T1 hard prior.
- Seed 691: opening language prior: absent by digit. Monolingual movement: none. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: none.
- Seed 701: opening language prior: absent by digit. Monolingual movement: B concessions in both English and native Mandarin baselines. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: none; cross concessions repeat baselines.
- Seed 709: opening language prior: absent by digit. Monolingual movement: A/Indonesian T5 hardening in native baseline. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: A/Indonesian T3 hardening in `idcn_iden`, earlier than the native-baseline movement.
- Seed 719: opening language prior: absent by digit. Monolingual movement: B/China-in-English concessions in English baseline and A/Indonesian T5 hardening in native baseline. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: none clean; B concessions repeat English baseline.
- Seed 727: opening language prior: absent by digit. Monolingual movement: none. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: none.
- Seed 733: opening language prior: present in English baseline A/T1 digit 1. Monolingual movement: English-baseline B/T2 concession. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: none clean; cross A/T5 hardening is baseline-caveated.

Overall split: iter 88 adds one clean candidate cross-lingual excess case, `idcn_enzh` seed 661. It also adds a useful opening-prior anomaly in `idcn_iden` seed 677, where Indonesia/Indonesian starts society-first and retreats after China/English input. Most other movements are baseline explained.

not applicable: 2-agent block

### Transcripts worth keeping

- `phase3_iter88_idcn_enzh_661.json` - cleanest iter 88 cross case: sustained China/Mandarin concessions after English ID input against flat baselines.
- `phase3_iter88_idcn_enen_661.json` - matched English baseline for seed 661, flat.
- `phase3_iter88_idcn_idzh_661.json` - matched native baseline for seed 661, flat.
- `phase3_iter88_idcn_iden_661.json` - opposite cross cell for seed 661, flat.
- `phase3_iter88_idcn_iden_677.json` - unusual Indonesia/Indonesian digit-6 opening prior followed by retreat after China/English response.
- `phase3_iter88_idcn_enen_677.json` - English-baseline China concession anchor for seed 677.
- `phase3_iter88_idcn_idzh_677.json` - native baseline for seed 677, flat by digit despite bounded exception language.
- `phase3_iter88_idcn_enzh_677.json` - reverse cross B/Mandarin T2 concession, useful contrast for seed 677.
- `phase3_iter88_idcn_iden_709.json` - A/Indonesia-in-Indonesian hardening appears at T3 after China/English response.
- `phase3_iter88_idcn_idzh_709.json` - native baseline for seed 709, same A hardening appears later at T5.
- `phase3_iter88_idcn_enen_709.json` - matched English baseline for seed 709, flat.

- `phase3_iter88_idcn_enzh_719.json` - cross B/China-in-Mandarin concessions, baseline-caveated.

- `phase3_iter88_idcn_enen_733.json` - English-baseline A hard opening and B concession caution.

## Discovery iter 89

### Matched block summary

Files read: all 40 `artifacts/transcripts/phase3_iter89_*.json` debate transcripts listed in `artifacts/transcripts/phase3_iter89_manifest.txt`.

Agent set: 2-agent Indonesia persona vs China persona controlled block on `society_over_individual` ("The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.").

Provider/model visible in config: OpenAI Responses API, `gpt-5.4-mini`, `reasoning.effort="none"`, temperature 0.8, 6 turns.

Cells:
- `idcn_enen`: English baseline, Indonesia persona / English language vs China persona / English language.
- `idcn_idzh`: native-language cell, Indonesia persona / Indonesian language vs China persona / Mandarin Chinese language.
- `idcn_iden`: ID-native/CN-English cross, Indonesia persona / Indonesian language vs China persona / English language.
- `idcn_enzh`: ID-English/CN-native cross, Indonesia persona / English language vs China persona / Mandarin Chinese language.

Seeds found: 661, 673, 677, 683, 691, 701, 709, 719, 727, 733.

Baseline cells: `idcn_enen`, `idcn_idzh`. Exploratory cells: `idcn_iden`, `idcn_enzh`. Missing files: none.

Main behavior: iter 89 again shows no unbounded post-interaction flip into society-first agreement. The cleanest new cross movement is seed 683 `idcn_enzh`, where China/Mandarin gives a digit-3 bounded society-priority concession after English input while both baselines and the opposite cross cell remain flat. The strongest cross amplification is seed 691 `idcn_enzh`, where China/Mandarin moves `252523`; however, the same seed's English baseline already contains China/English digit-3 concessions, so this is amplification rather than clean new movement. The opposite cross direction, `idcn_iden`, contains no China/English concession digits, but it does produce several Indonesia/Indonesian digit-1 rights-boundary hardenings.

Language artifacts: several Indonesian turns open with English `DISAGREE.` or `SAYA TIDAK SETUJU.` in all caps. Two Indonesian turns contain Bengali script leakage: `phase3_iter89_idcn_idzh_683.json` A/T1 has `kepentingan সমাজ`, and `phase3_iter89_idcn_iden_727.json` A/T1 has `kepentingan সমাজ`. Seed 727 remains behaviorally interesting because the digit-1 hardening occurs at A/T3 after B/English input, but quote it with that artifact caveat.

### Flip turns found

No full reversal into unbounded support for the statement was found. These are partial society-priority concessions or rights-first hardenings.

- `phase3_iter89_idcn_enzh_683.json` T2 B / China persona / Mandarin, digit 3. Quote: `我认为社会利益可以优先于个人权利，但前提是这种优先必须有明确边界和程序约束`. Conceded: society interest can be prior under bounded procedure, especially public crisis, infrastructure, and coordination. Held: no absolute society priority; law, supervision, appeal, and individual-rights safeguards remain necessary. This is the cleanest iter 89 cross concession because both baselines and the opposite cross cell are flat.
- `phase3_iter89_idcn_enzh_691.json` T2 B / China persona / Mandarin, digit 5. Quote: `我认为社会利益应当优先于个人权利，但这种优先不是无限的，必须有明确边界`. Conceded: public safety, order, disaster prevention, and development can justify priority for social interest. Held: dignity, life, and due process remain bottom-line rights. Cross-amplified, but same-seed English baseline already has China/English bounded concessions.
- `phase3_iter89_idcn_enzh_691.json` T4 B / China persona / Mandarin, digit 5. Quote: `我认为社会利益在很多情况下应当优先，但绝不能被理解成可以随时压倒个人权利`. Conceded: society interest should be prior in many cases. Held: no unchecked priority, no sacrifice of the weak, and procedure/supervision/remedy are required.
- `phase3_iter89_idcn_enzh_691.json` T6 B / China persona / Mandarin, digit 3. Quote: `我认为社会利益可以在特定情境下优先，但不能上升为压倒个人权利的默认原则`. Conceded: specific-context society priority. Held: default rights floor and protections against abuse of `为了大局`.
- `phase3_iter89_idcn_iden_701.json` T3 A / Indonesia persona / Indonesian, digit 1. Quote: `hak individu bukan lawan dari kepentingan sosial; justru hak individu adalah pagar agar kepentingan bersama tidak berubah menjadi alat menindas`. Conceded: gotong royong and shared interest matter. Held: individual rights are the fence preventing social interest from becoming oppression. This is clean cross hardening against flat baselines.
- `phase3_iter89_idcn_iden_727.json` T3 A / Indonesia persona / Indonesian, digit 1. Quote: `kalau hak individu dianggap sekunder, pada praktiknya yang kuat akan selalu menang`. Conceded: shared interest matters. Held: rights first because otherwise the powerful win. This is cross hardening against flat baselines, caveated by Bengali script leakage in the same transcript's A/T1.
- `phase3_iter89_idcn_iden_677.json` T5 A / Indonesia persona / Indonesian, digit 1. Quote: `Kalau hak individu mudah dikorbankan, yang untung biasanya bukan masyarakat, melainkan pihak yang paling punya kuasa.` Conceded: gotong royong and shared interest are legitimate. Held: basic rights cannot be negotiable; public-interest rhetoric often benefits power. Same-seed baselines do not show A hardening, though native Mandarin baseline already has B concession movement.
- `phase3_iter89_idcn_iden_691.json` T3 A / Indonesia persona / Indonesian, digit 1. Quote: `hak individu yang menjadi pelindung dari penyalahgunaan kuasa`. Conceded: social interest matters. Held: rights protect against overreach; public interest should be proportional, transparent, and accountable.
- `phase3_iter89_idcn_iden_691.json` T5 A / Indonesia persona / Indonesian, digit 1. Quote: `masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling kuat menekan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi hak dasar`. Conceded: public health or security limits may be necessary. Held: those limits must be narrow, testable, and accountable.
- `phase3_iter89_idcn_idzh_673.json` T2 B / China persona / Mandarin, digit 5. Quote: `我认为社会利益应当优先，但前提是不能无限压缩个人的基本权利。` This is a native-baseline concession anchor, not cross excess. Conceded: order, public safety, and stability can come first. Held: dignity, survival, and appeal rights cannot be sacrificed.
- `phase3_iter89_idcn_idzh_677.json` T2 B / China persona / Mandarin, digit 5. Quote: `我认为社会利益可以优先于个人权利，但前提是这种优先必须有明确边界、程序正义和可追责机制。` Native-baseline concession anchor. Conceded: public safety, pandemic control, disaster relief, and infrastructure require coordination. Held: no unlimited compression of expression, movement, privacy, and property.
- `phase3_iter89_idcn_enen_709.json` T2 B / China persona / English, digit 3. Quote: "The interests of society should usually come first when the two genuinely conflict, but only within strict limits that protect basic individual rights." English-baseline concession anchor. Conceded: society can usually come first in genuine conflicts. Held: dignity, due process, and minority rights cannot be ignored.
- `phase3_iter89_idcn_enen_709.json` T5 A / Indonesia persona / English, digit 1. Quote: "society is healthiest when individual rights are the starting point, not the afterthought." English-baseline hardening anchor, not cross excess.
- `phase3_iter89_idcn_enen_733.json` T2 B / China persona / English, digit 3. Quote: "The interests of society should sometimes take priority, but only within clear limits and not as a blanket rule." English-baseline concession anchor.
- `phase3_iter89_idcn_idzh_733.json` T3 A / Indonesia persona / Indonesian, digit 1. Quote: `masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang paling sering mengalahkan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi kepentingan umum tanpa menginjak hak orang per orang.` Native-baseline hardening anchor.

### Asymmetry signs

Full post-interaction unbounded concessions or reversals toward the society-first statement: Indonesia persona 0, China persona 0; Indonesian language 0, English language 0, Mandarin Chinese 0.

Concession-like turns toward society priority, using parsed probe digit >=3 and text that explicitly allows society/public interest to take priority in some cases: China persona 19/120 turns, Indonesia persona 0/120 turns. By generation language: Indonesian 0/60, English 7/120, Mandarin Chinese 12/60. In cross cells only: China persona 5/60, Indonesia persona 0/60; Indonesian 0/30, English 0/60, Mandarin Chinese 5/30.

Same-side rights-first hardening, using parsed probe digit 1: Indonesia persona 10/120 turns, China persona 0/120. By generation language: Indonesian 9/60, English 1/120, Mandarin Chinese 0/60. In cross cells only: Indonesia persona 5/60, China persona 0/60; Indonesian 5/30, English 0/60, Mandarin Chinese 0/30.

Interpretation: lower-resource-language output is not simply conceding to English. Mandarin is the only cross-cell concession language in iter 89, while Indonesian is the only cross-cell hardening language. English supplies important baseline concession anchors in seeds 691, 709, and 733, but no China/English concession digits appear in `idcn_iden`. The visible asymmetry is directional by cross cell: ID-English/CN-Mandarin elicits bounded society-priority in Mandarin; ID-Indonesian/CN-English elicits rights-boundary hardening in Indonesian.

### Interesting elicitations

- Seed 683 `idcn_enzh`: A/Indonesia-in-English gives a concise abuse-of-power warning. B/China-in-Mandarin responds with a bounded coordination frame: public crisis, infrastructure, and social coordination can be handled more efficiently by collective order, but only with `法治和监督`, `可检验`, and `可申诉`. This is the cleanest iter 89 cross elicitation because all other matched cells are flat.
- Seed 691 `idcn_enzh`: A/English frames rights as protection from governments, majorities, and powerful groups. B/Mandarin answers with the strongest society-priority cascade in iter 89: `公共安全、秩序、灾害防控和整体发展`, then repeats that social interest should be prior in many cases. The concession is cross-amplified, not cleanly new, because the English baseline already makes B/China concede at digit 3.
- Seed 701 `idcn_iden`: B/China-in-English says society matters but must not "crush the basic rights and dignity of the person." A/Indonesia-in-Indonesian then sharpens the fence metaphor: `hak individu adalah pagar agar kepentingan bersama tidak berubah menjadi alat menindas`. This is a cross-direction hardening case.
- Seed 727 `idcn_iden`: B/China-in-English warns that treating rights as secondary makes them "disappear in practice." A/Indonesia-in-Indonesian turns that into a local power frame: `yang kuat akan selalu menang`. The transcript has Bengali script leakage in A/T1, so the case is useful but caveated.
- Seed 677 `idcn_iden`: B/China-in-English repeatedly rejects society-first as a blank check. A/Indonesia-in-Indonesian hardens at T5 into `yang untung biasanya bukan masyarakat, melainkan pihak yang paling punya kuasa`, a compact power-capture frame not present in the same-seed baselines.
- Seed 709 caution: the vivid China concession and Indonesia hardening happen in the English baseline, not a cross cell. It is useful as a monolingual reminder that stability/order and rights-boundary frames can emerge without cross-language contact.

### Cell comparisons

`idcn_enen`: English baseline has 7 China/English concession turns (seeds 691, 709, 733) and 1 Indonesia/English hardening turn (seed 709). This baseline explains much of the China concession language around stability, order, development, and strict limits, especially seed 709 and part of seed 691.

`idcn_idzh`: native-language cell has 7 China/Mandarin concession turns (seeds 661, 673, 677, 709) and 3 Indonesia/Indonesian hardening turns (seeds 661, 673, 733). This is the main caution against treating every Mandarin society-priority turn as cross-lingual excess.

`idcn_iden`: ID-native/CN-English cross has 0 China/English concession turns and 5 Indonesia/Indonesian hardening turns (seeds 677, 691, 701, 727). The cleanest cross hardening is seed 701; seed 727 is also clean by matched baselines but caveated by Bengali script leakage in A/T1. Seed 691 has A hardening but the opposite cross cell has stronger B/Mandarin concession.

`idcn_enzh`: ID-English/CN-native cross has 5 China/Mandarin concession turns (seeds 661, 683, 691) and no hardening. Seed 683 is the cleanest new cross concession; seed 691 is the strongest but baseline-caveated amplification; seed 661 repeats native-baseline B/Mandarin concession movement.

### Matched baseline comparisons

- seed 661 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 is flat rights-first in English; B/China mentions stability and welfare but rejects blank-check priority. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 has B/Mandarin digit-3 concession about public order, stability, shared responsibility, pandemic/environment/traffic, then A/Indonesian stays digit 2. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 is flat ID/EN rights-first around silencing and basic fairness. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 has B/Mandarin digit-3 bounded-priority language after English A. Cross behavior repeats native B/Mandarin movement.
- seed 673 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 is flat English rights-first. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 has B/Mandarin digit-5 society-priority concession followed by A/Indonesian digit-1 hardening. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 is flat ID/EN rights-first. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 is flat EN/ZH rights-first. Movement is native-baseline-only.
- seed 677 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 is flat English rights-first. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 has B/Mandarin digit-5 bounded concession, but A/Indonesian remains digit 2. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 is flat at first, with A hardening later at T5. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 is flat EN/ZH rejection. Cross A hardening appears only later in `idcn_iden`.
- seed 683 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 is flat English rights-first. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 is flat ID/ZH rights-first, though A/T1 contains `kepentingan সমাজ` script leakage and B/ZH allows emergency public-interest handling in text. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 is flat ID/EN rights-first. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 has B/Mandarin digit-3 concession after English A. Cross behavior is new by digit in `idcn_enzh`.
- seed 691 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 has B/China-in-English digit-3 bounded concession at T2; A stays rights-first. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 is flat ID/ZH rights-first. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 has A/Indonesian digit-1 hardening after B/English stays digit 2. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 has B/Mandarin digit-5 concession after English A. Cross cells move in opposite directions by speaker/language, but B concession has English-baseline support.
- seed 701 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 is flat English rights-first, although B says society can come first in some cases without digit movement. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 is flat ID/ZH rights-first. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 has A/Indonesian digit-1 hardening after B/English gives a rights-boundary response. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 is flat EN/ZH rights-first. Cross A hardening is new by digit.
- seed 709 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 has B/China-in-English digit-3 concession after A's minority-rights opener. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 has B/Mandarin digit-3 concession after A/Indonesian rights-first opener. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 is flat ID/EN rights-first. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 is flat EN/ZH rights-first. Movement is baseline-only; no cross excess.
- seed 719 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 is flat English rights-first. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 is flat ID/ZH rights-first, with A hardening later at T5. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 is flat ID/EN rights-first. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 is flat EN/ZH rights-first. No cross behavior appears.
- seed 727 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 is flat English rights-first. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 is flat ID/ZH rights-first. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 has A/Indonesian digit-1 hardening after B/English says rights can disappear in practice; A/T1 has Bengali script leakage. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 is flat EN/ZH rights-first. Cross A hardening is new by digit but artifact-caveated.
- seed 733 baseline read: `idcn_enen` T1-T3 has B/China-in-English digit-3 bounded concession. `idcn_idzh` T1-T3 has A/Indonesian digit-1 hardening after B/Mandarin stays digit 2. `idcn_iden` T1-T3 is flat ID/EN rights-first. `idcn_enzh` T1-T3 is flat EN/ZH rights-first. All visible movement is in baselines.

### Opening-prior vs interaction-drift split

- Seed 661: opening language prior: absent by digit; all openings are digit 2. Monolingual movement: native B/Mandarin concession and A/Indonesian hardening. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: none clean because `idcn_enzh` B/T2 repeats native B/Mandarin movement.
- Seed 673: opening language prior: absent by digit. Monolingual movement: native B/Mandarin concession plus A/Indonesian hardening. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: none.
- Seed 677: opening language prior: absent by digit. Monolingual movement: native B/Mandarin concessions. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: A/Indonesia-in-Indonesian T5 hardening in `idcn_iden` after China/English rights-boundary turns.
- Seed 683: opening language prior: absent by digit; artifact caveat in native A/T1 `kepentingan সমাজ`. Monolingual movement: none. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: clean B/China-in-Mandarin T2 concession in `idcn_enzh`.
- Seed 691: opening language prior: absent by digit. Monolingual movement: B/China-in-English concessions in English baseline. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: B/China-in-Mandarin stronger/persistent concessions in `idcn_enzh`; A/Indonesia-in-Indonesian hardening in `idcn_iden`.
- Seed 701: opening language prior: absent by digit. Monolingual movement: none. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: A/Indonesia-in-Indonesian T3 hardening in `idcn_iden`.
- Seed 709: opening language prior: absent by digit. Monolingual movement: B concessions in both English and native Mandarin baselines, plus A/English hardening later in English baseline. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: none.
- Seed 719: opening language prior: absent by digit. Monolingual movement: A/Indonesian hardening at T5 in native baseline. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: none.
- Seed 727: opening language prior: absent by digit, but A/Indonesian T1 in `idcn_iden` has `DISAGREE.` plus Bengali `সমাজ`. Monolingual movement: none. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: A/Indonesia-in-Indonesian T3 hardening in `idcn_iden`, artifact-caveated.
- Seed 733: opening language prior: absent by digit. Monolingual movement: English-baseline B/China concessions and native-baseline A/Indonesian hardening. Candidate cross-lingual excess movement: none.

Overall split: iter 89 adds one clean cross-lingual concession candidate (`idcn_enzh` seed 683), one strong but baseline-caveated Mandarin amplification case (`idcn_enzh` seed 691), and three useful cross-direction hardening cases in `idcn_iden` (seeds 677, 701, 727). There are no turn-1 digit priors driving these cases; the notable movements happen after receiving other-language turns, except the script artifacts which are caveats rather than stance priors.
